fact_nuggets.text: Compound Eyes (Japanese: Compound Eyes), formatted as Compoundeyes prior to Pokemon X and Y, is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Boosts the Accuracy of moves. MD T D S Boosts the Pokemon's accuracy. B S L Conq. The Pokemon's accuracy is boosted. MD GtI Accuracy goes up. * Accuracy goes up! * S MD The Pokemon's accuracy goes up! MD RT DX The Pokemon's accuracy goes up.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Affected moves 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Compound Eyes increases the accuracy of moves used by the Pokemon with this Ability by multiplying the original accuracy by a factor of 1.3. This means that Thunder , for example, has 91% accuracy as opposed to the usual 70% (70 1.3 = 91%). Starting in Generation V, this multiplier is approximated as 5325/4096. It does not affect one-hit knockout moves . Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Compound Eyes, as well as the Pokemon that can have Compound Eyes and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Pow. edit source ] From Pokemon Emerald onward, if a Pokemon with this Ability leads the party (even if fainted ), the chances of finding a wild Pokemon holding an item increase from 50%/5% to 60%/20%. In dark grass in Generation V , the chances increase from 50%/5%/1% to 60%/20%/5%. In Generation VIII , this effect is shared with Super Luck . Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Scarlet and Violet , Compound Eyes has no effect on the items held by wild Pokemon.

In battle [ edit | edit source ] Compound Eyes increases the accuracy of moves used by the Pokemon with this Ability by multiplying the original accuracy by a factor of 1.3. This means that Thunder , for example, has 91% accuracy as opposed to the usual 70% (70 1.3 = 91%). Starting in Generation V, this multiplier is approximated as 5325/4096. It does not affect one-hit knockout moves . Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Compound Eyes, as well as the Pokemon that can have Compound Eyes and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Pow.
